introduction: In Delhi, marriage registration is carried out under the Hindu Marriage Act or the Special Marriage Act through the Revenue Department's e-District portal. It provides conclusive proof of a legally recognized union.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Processing Time: 15-30 days post physical verification.
  • Application Fee: ₹100 for Hindu Marriage Act, ₹150 for Special Marriage Act. Penalty applies for late registration (after 60 days).
  • A Marriage Certificate is valid for a lifetime.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Marriage Registration

Note: You need to provide documents for Proof of Age, Proof of Address, Photographs & Evidence, and Witnesses.
Proof of Identity & Age
  • Birth Certificate
  • Class 10th Certificate
  • Passport
Proof of Address
  • Voter ID
  • Passport
  • Ration Card or Driving License
Evidence of Marriage & Affidavit
  • 2 passport-size photographs of bride & groom
  • 2 photographs of the marriage ceremony
  • Wedding invitation card
  • Joint affidavit in the prescribed format
Witnesses
  • At least two witnesses
  • Witness Pan Card/Aadhaar and Address Proof
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply for Marriage Certificate Online

The e-District portal provides a unified approach to uploading documents and booking SDM appointments in Delhi.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit e-District Delhi: Go to the official e-District Delhi Portal.
  2. Visit e-District Delhi Portal
    Go to edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in. This is the official Government of Delhi citizen services portal.
  3. Register / Login
    Click "New User", enter your Aadhaar number and mobile number and verify via OTP. Existing users can log in with their user ID and password.
  4. Select Marriage Registration Service
    From the dashboard click "Apply for Services" → "Revenue Department" → "Registration of Marriage (Hindu Marriage Act)" or Special Marriage Act as applicable.
  5. Fill the Application Form
    Enter both spouses' full names (as per ID), date and place of marriage, addresses, Aadhaar numbers, and witness details.
  6. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies (JPG/PDF, max 100 KB each): Birth Certificate/Class 10 marksheet, Aadhaar, address proof, 2 passport-size photos of each spouse, 2 ceremony photos, wedding invitation, and joint affidavit.
  7. Book SDM Appointment
    Select your Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) office and book an available slot. An acknowledgement slip is generated — save and print it.
  8. Attend SDM Office on Appointment Date
    Both spouses and at least 2 witnesses must appear with all original documents. The SDM verifies identity and registers the marriage.
Download Certificate: After approval, download the signed Marriage Certificate from e-District Delhi using your application number.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at SDM Office

Visit the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) office in the district where either spouse has resided for at least 6 months. Working hours: Monday – Friday, 9:30 AM – 6:00 PM.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Find the Right SDM Office
    Identify the SDM office based on the residential address of either spouse. A list of Delhi SDM offices is available at revenue.delhi.gov.in.
  2. Collect the Application Form
    Obtain the Marriage Registration form from the SDM office counter. It is available free of cost.
  3. Fill All Details Accurately
    Enter names exactly as on Aadhaar/ID proof, marriage date and place, addresses of both spouses, and names and addresses of at least 2 witnesses.
  4. Prepare a Joint Notarized Affidavit
    Get a joint affidavit sworn before a Notary or First Class Magistrate declaring the marriage date, place, marital status, nationality, and religion. This is mandatory.
  5. Attach Self-Attested Copies
    Attach: Birth Certificate/Class 10 marksheet, Aadhaar, address proof, 2 passport-size photos each, 2 ceremony photos, wedding invitation, and witness ID proofs. Bring originals.
  6. Submit at Counter & Pay Fee
    Submit the completed form and documents. Pay ₹100 (Hindu Marriage Act) or ₹150 (Special Marriage Act) at the cash counter. Collect the Acknowledgement Receipt.
  7. SDM Verification & Certificate
    The SDM verifies all documents and witnesses in person. After sign-off, the certificate is issued within 15–30 working days or can be downloaded from e-District Delhi.
Processing Time: 15–30 working days. Delhi helpline: 1076 (CM Helpline) or 011-23935730 (Revenue Dept).
afterApplying:
After Registration - What Next?

SDM Visit

Both spouses must be physically present at the SDM office on the scheduled date alongside their requested witnesses. Wait time at the office can range up to a few hours.

Certificate Download

The approved marriage certificate can later be fully downloaded via the e-District portal online securely.
